title: FERULIC ACID
description: Ferulic acid is a phenolic compound found abundantly in plant cell walls, particularly in cereals, grains, fruits, and vegetables. It functions primarily as a potent antioxidant by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, while also exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and potential anticancer properties. It is commonly used in skincare products, food preservation, and as a dietary supplement for its cardiovascular and neuroprotective benefits.

research.title: Ferulic Acid: A Comprehensive Review
research.summary: Comprehensive analysis showing ferulic acid demonstrates significant antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties, though clinical application is limited by poor water solubility and bioavailability challenges.

research.title: Ferulic Acid Supplementation Improves Lipid Profiles, Oxidative Stress, and Inflammatory Status in Hyperlipidemic Subjects
research.summary: Randomized controlled trial found 1000mg daily ferulic acid significantly decreased total cholesterol (8.1%), LDL-C (9.3%), oxidative stress markers (24.5%), and inflammatory markers including hs-CRP (32.66%) in hyperlipidemic subjects over six weeks.

research.title: Ferulic Acid: Extraction, Estimation, Bioactivity and Applications for Human Health and Food
research.summary: Review demonstrating ferulic acid exhibits antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, anticancer, antidiabetic, cardiovascular and neuroprotective activities with applications in food preservation, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics industries.

sentiment.summary: Limited direct user discussion of ferulic acid supplementation, with most mentions appearing in broader anti-aging and antioxidant contexts. Scientific evidence shows promising cardiovascular and anti-inflammatory benefits, but user experiences are not extensively documented in the provided Reddit data.

effects.label: Clinical Evidence:
effects.description: Well-supported by randomized controlled trials showing measurable improvements in lipid profiles within 6 weeks

effects.label: Bioavailability Challenge:
effects.description: Poor water solubility limits absorption, though it's absorbed better than some other phenolic acids

effects.label: Dosage Response:
effects.description: 1000mg daily appears effective for cardiovascular benefits in clinical studies

effects.label: Synergistic Potential:
effects.description: Often used in combination with other antioxidants in skincare and supplement formulations

dosage.label: Standard Dose:
dosage.description: 1000mg daily used in clinical trials for cardiovascular and anti-inflammatory benefits

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Can be absorbed in small intestine and eliminated through urine, suggesting flexible dosing schedules

dosage.label: Food Sources:
dosage.description: Naturally abundant in whole grains, fruits, vegetables, and coffee as dietary intake option

dosage.label: Supplement Forms:
dosage.description: Available as standalone supplement or combined in antioxidant formulations

sideEffects.label: Gastric Acid Concern:
sideEffects.description: May be degraded or weakened by stomach acid, potentially reducing effectiveness

sideEffects.label: Limited Toxicity Data:
sideEffects.description: Noted as non-toxic compound in research, but long-term human safety data is limited

sideEffects.label: Bioavailability Issues:
sideEffects.description: Poor water solubility may limit absorption and therapeutic effects

sideEffects.label: Interaction Potential:
sideEffects.description: As with phenolic compounds, may interact with certain medications, though specific interactions not well documented

availability.label: Food Applications:
availability.description: Widely used in food industry as preservative, additive, and in edible films for packaging

availability.label: Supplement Market:
availability.description: Available as dietary supplement, though less commonly discussed than other antioxidants in user communities

availability.label: Natural Sources:
availability.description: Easily obtained through diet via cereals, grains, vegetables, fruits, and coffee consumption
